title: Bridge Essay: Does socioeconomic position and gender affect human-nature interactions?

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Ecology/Animism — nature connectedness, biophilia, environmental neuroscience.
**Phase context:** Nature connectedness, biophilia, environmental neuroscience.

**Does socioeconomic position and gender affect human-nature interactions?** — Z Gesundh Wiss (2022). DOI: 10.1007/s10389-022-01762-8. PMID: 36277027. AIM: The growing number of mental health problems worldwide is alarming. Encouraging human-nature interactions (HNIs) could help to tackle this issue. For this reason, the aim of the present research was to investigate certain components that promote HNI in two groups of students with different socioeconomic positions (SEPs) in Mexico. HNIs describe the direct relationship between humans and nature. HNIs are composed of elements such as connectedness to nature (CN) and commitment to the environm
